learning_outcomes.description: Identify different features of pragmatic meaning
Understand the systematic ways in which language can be used to deliver actions
Effectively apply the concepts and theories studied in this course to the analysis of language use across different contexts
Critically reflect on the implications of the concepts and theories studied in this course for the learning and teaching of second languages

WEEKS 2 to 7 and 9 to 12. Each quiz is made available on Wednesday and must be submitted by 5pm on Friday of the same week.
assessments.task_description:
Each week, from WEEK 2 to 7 and 9 to 12, students will need to complete an online Weekly Quiz consisting of 3 questions. These quizzes will be available every Wednesday on Blackboard and must be submitted by 5pm on Friday of the same week. The questions will cover the lecture material and required readings for that week. Each Weekly Quiz will contribute 3% to the final grade. After completing all ten quizzes, the total score will be calculated. Answers to the questions will be marked as correct or incorrect. For each correct answer, students will receive 1 point. Each Weekly Quiz consists of 3 questions, and there are 10 quizzes in total, meaning students can earn up to 30 points if they answer all questions correctly.

STATEMENT ON AI & MT

Artificial Intelligence (AI) and Machine Translation (MT) are emerging tools that may support students in completing this assessment task. Students may appropriately use AI and/or MT in completing this assessment task. Students must clearly reference any use of AI or MT in each instance. A failure to reference generative AI or MT use may constitute student misconduct under the Student Code of Conduct.
assessments.submission_guidelines: The weekly online study tasks will need to be accessed, completed, and submitted via Blackboard/UQ Learn.
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You cannot defer or apply for an extension for this assessment.
assessments.late_submission: A penalty of 10% of the maximum possible mark will be deducted per 24 hours from time submission is due for up to 7 days. After 7 days, you will receive a mark of 0.
assessments.additional_info: You cannot defer or apply for an extension for this assessment.

assessments.task_description:
This is an in-semester examination held during the WEEK 8 Lecture. It will cover the course content studied in WEEKS 1 to 7. You will be asked to answer short answer questions and will be given 60 minutes (no planning time) to complete it.

STATEMENT ON AI & MT

This assessment task is to be completed in-person. The use of generative Artificial Intelligence (AI) or Machine Translation (MT) tools will not be permitted. Any attempted use of AI or MT may constitute student misconduct under the Student Code of Conduct.
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You may be able to defer this exam.
assessments.late_submission: A penalty of 10% of the maximum possible mark will be deducted per 24 hours from time submission is due for up to 7 days. After 7 days, you will receive a mark of 0.
assessments.additional_info: You may be able to defer this exam.

assessments.due_date:
26/10/2026 - 30/10/2026

Video Submission - Monday WEEK 13, 5pm

Individual Interview - during Week 13 tutorials (Thursday and Friday)
assessments.mode: Activity/ Performance, Oral, Product/ Artefact/ Multimedia
assessments.task_description:
This assessment item involves two parts.

Part I: Video Presentation (20 marks).

You will record a 10-minute presentation in which you critically analyse one pragmatic phenomenon examined in this course.

You should:

  • Clearly define and contextualise the chosen concept (e.g., implicature, reference, speech acts, etc.)
  • Engage with relevant theories and key readings from the course
  • Provide and analyse authentic or constructed examples
  • Critically evaluate how meaning is shaped in context (e.g., social, cultural, interactional factors)

You must upload this video by Monday 5:00pm in Week 13.

Part II: Tutorial Interview (20 marks).

In Week 13, you will attend your tutorial and participate in a short individual interview with your tutor about your presentation.

During this interview, you will be asked questions designed to:

  • Check your understanding of the key concepts and arguments in your presentation
  • Clarify and extend your analysis
  • Reflect on relevant theoretical perspectives and readings

This is a closed-book and no-device 5-minute interview. Your responses should demonstrate:

  • Satisfactory understanding of the selected topic (10%)
  • Capacity to articulate and justify your ideas clearly without reliance on resources (10%)

STATEMENT ON AI & MT

Students may appropriately use AI and/or MT in completing this assessment task. Students must clearly reference any use of AI or MT in each instance. A failure to reference generative AI or MT use may constitute student misconduct under the Student Code of Conduct.

The use of generative AI or MT tools in the in-person component of this assessment will not be permitted. Any attempted use of AI or MT may constitute student misconduct under the Student Code of Conduct.
